title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption: Horizontal bar charts display 2020 index scores for global citizenship and sustainable development education across four countries, measuring the extent to which these topics are mainstreamed into national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula on a scale from 0 to 1. Colombia and India both show maximum scores of 1 for national education policies and student assessment, while Malawi matches these top scores in the same two categories. India extends high performance to teacher education at 0.95 and education curricula at 0.92. Malawi follows with 0.91 for education curricula and 0.9 for teacher education. Colombia records 0.88 for education curricula and 0.85 for teacher education. Bulgaria presents lower scores across all four strands, ranging from 0.73 for teacher education down to 0.56 for national education policies, with student assessment at 0.71 and education curricula at 0.65. The bars show a clear separation between the three countries with multiple scores at or above 0.85 and Bulgaria, where all four indicators fall below 0.75.
